lines.text: Part 1 written by: Shana Goldberg-Meehan & Scott Silveri
Part 2 written by: Marta Kauffman & David Crane
Central Perk

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey! I'm all packed and ready to go!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, that's right! (to Emma) Daddy and uncle Joey are going on a trip today. We're going to a conference in Barbados, right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Mmh-mmh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Emma) Can you say Barbados?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Barbados!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, I gotta say. I mean, it means so much to me that you guys are coming all the way over there to hear me do my speech! UH! And I've a surprise, uh... I had to pull some strings but I was able to get everyone passes to the entire conference! That's right! (he gives them their passes) This babies will get you into all the paleontology lectures and seminars.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you have anything that would... get us out of them?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah Ross, I mean... we're excited to hear the speech but the rest of the time we're gonna wanna do, you know, "island's stuff".

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I think David would probably wanna hear a few lectures.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, right, because he's a scientist!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, because, you know, he's been in Minsk for 8 years and if he gets too much direct sunlight, he'll die.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, we gotta go, yeah? So, we'll see you guys tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, let's do it! 5 hour flight with Charlie, have a couple of drinks, get under that blanket and do what comes naturally.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's a blanket Joe, not a cloak of invisibility!
Opening credits
Paradise Hotel lounge in Barbados

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Wow! This place is beautiful!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (very excited) Look at all these paleontologists!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know, there are gonna be some pasty folks by the pool tomorrow! (A woman goes towards them)

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Oh my God, I can't believe you're here!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Charlie) I think I've been recognized, this happens all the time!

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Doctor Geller, I'm such a huge fan!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That... never happens...

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I've been following your career for years, I-I can't wait for your keynote speech.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! This is very flattering, uh...

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I would love your autograph. (hands him a notepad)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, uh... Sure! Uhm... "Dear..." (he takes the notepad)

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Sarah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: "... Sarah. I dig you", Uh? "Doctor Ross Geller".

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Thank you so much!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, oh and Sarah... I'd like to introduce you to my colleague, uh, Professor Wheeler, a-and this is Joey Tribbiani.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: (to Joey) Are you a paleontologist?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, God, no! No! No no, I'm an actor. You'd probably recognize me from a little show called "The Days of Our Lives".

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dude, it's just "Days of Our Lives"... there's no the.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (thinking he's kidding) Ok, Ross! It's... It's fun, yeah! No, I-I play Doctor Drake Ramoray.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: I'm sorry, I don't own a TV.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You don't own a TV? What's all your furniture pointed at??
Central Perk

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: David, can you help me?! I'm trying to explain to Chandler how a plane stays in the air.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Oh, certainly. That's a combination of Bernoulli's principle and Newton's third law of motion.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Chandler) See?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, that's the same as "it has something to do with wind".

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright, I'm gonna go pick up a few things for the trip.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I should go, too. Oh, now... tomorrow do you guys wanna share a cab to the airport or should Mike and I just meet you there.
Everyone looks at her

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mike?? Who's Mike?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Mike is your ex... uh... boyfriend!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's right! Oh, yeah... Well, I've totally forgotten about im! AH! That's-That's... a blast from the past!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: It's ok. Ho-honest mistake.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really, it doesn't mean anything. I mean, you know, Monica refers to Chandler as Richard all the time!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (upset) She does?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (pinching her) Let's get you out of here!! (they go outside)
Outside the Central Perk

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: At least you took me down with you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sooo sorry!! I just... I keep thinking about Mike! I'm crazy about David, and we're having so much fun together. Why-Why do I miss Mike? That's-that's gonna go away, right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I guess, in time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I mean, my feelings for Richard are certainly gone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You just did it again. Chandler, your feelings for Chandler are certainly gone!
Inside Central Perk

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: (to Chandler) Well, Phoebe's still pretty hung up on that Mike, uh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I wouldn't read too much into it.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Still you know, a girl calls you by your ex-boyfriend's name, that-that's not a good thing, right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: David, let me stop you there 'cause I think I see where this is going. I'm not very good at giving advice. So if you want advice, go to Ross, Monica, or... Joey, if the thing you wanna advice about is pizza toppings or burning sensation when you pee.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Sorry, I just... I wish there was something I could do, you know? Well, you know Phoebe...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Seriously, we're gonna do this?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: I'm sorry, uh... I just wish I could make her forget about Mike already, you know... Why did Phoebe and Mike break up?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, because his penis was too big. (he notices that David is not amused) Oh, I'm sorry, that's the kind of thing I do. (pause) They broke up because Mike didn't want to get married. Hey, what if you just let Phoebe know you'd be open to marriage?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: That's great! That's great! I-I'll propose to her!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Well, I was probably going to do it at some point.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I didn't mean now...

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Why not? It's brilliant! (talking to an imaginary Mike) Goodbye Mike, we'll see you at the wedding, fella! (pause) well, we probably won't invite you to the wedding... (to Chandler) Thank you, Chandler. Sincerely.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you're welcome! Glad I could help.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: (after a while) How do you think I should propose?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: David, I'm pretending to read here!!
Joey in his hotel in room in Barbados
Trying on a hat and talking to his own reflection in the mirror

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! How you doin'? Yeah alright!
Charlie comes out the bathroom

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, hey! You said you're gonna wear a thong, where's the thong?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: (laughing) I didn't mean a thong... I meant thongs...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You really should have been more clear about that!
Someone knocks the door, Joey goes to open it and Ross is on the other side

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Excited) You're never going to guess who I just saw downstairs!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! ah! eh... Britney Spears!?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, she never misses these conferences! (then to Charlie) No, I just saw Dr. Kenneth Schwartz!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh my God! Did you talk to him?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah... what am I going to say to Kenneth Schwartz?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You could say: "Hey Kenny, how come you're not Britney Spears?" (looks at Ross matter-of-factly)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Charlie) Ready to go?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wha...? You're gonna go now? I thought we could hang out?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh I can't... I have seminars all day and I promised Ross I would look at his speech.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: But maybe we can have dinner later? On the balcony? Will be romantic.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (smiling) Will you wear a thong?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I will if you will.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh... you got yourself a very weird deal!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (a little embarassed by their conversation) I'm good, I have dinner plans (moves away from them).

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: So you'll be ok?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah. I've got tons of stuff I could do. I'm gonna hit the beach, go swimming...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, Joe, have you looked outside?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, why?
Ross goes to the window and opens the curtains revealing that it's raining outside

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh man!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: There's an indoor pool, you can swim there! (Ross agrees)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I wasn't gonna swim, I was gonna dig a hole! (removes a small plastic spade used by children to play on the beach from his backpack)
Back in New York, Monica and Chandler in Central Perk on the couch

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! That Mike thing was interesting! I don't know what's gonna happen with Phoebe and David.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (smiling cheekily) I do! Want a hint? huh? "I do" (Monica looks confused, so Chandler repeats) "I do".

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, I'm sensing that this is some kind of word play, because you are pink with barely controlled glee.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: David is going to propose to Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? (looks very shocked) Why?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Be-cause, we were talking about ways that he could beat Mike and I told him that Phoebe wanted to get married.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, we have talked about this. You are not supposed to give people advice! Now couldn't you just have made some sort of inappropriate joke?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I did! A penis one! Look, just so I know, what was so wrong about what I said?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They've only been going out for a few weeks and Phoebe is completely hung up on Mike! She'll say "No", David's heart will be broken, it will be too hard for them to recover from and then Phoebe will end up alone again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Man, that's some bad advice!
Barbados, hotel lounge. David, Phoebe and Rachel have just arrived.
Joey spots them and walks towards them

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! Hey! Thank God you guys are here!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Hey what's going on?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Everything is upside down here! It rains all day long, nobody watches tv and Ross is famous!
Rachel turns around and sees Chandler and Monica arriving

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright, I don't wanna alarm anybody, but Monica's hair is twice as big as it was when we landed!
Monica and Chandler reach the group

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok! When I go places with high humidity, it gets a little extra body, ok?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's why our honeymoon photos look like me and Diana Ross!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on, I'll show you guys where to check in (Joey, Chandler and David leave)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Chandler) Oh, honey, can you make sure we get a King size bed!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (shouts after David) Oh! David, get one for us too! Oh, oh, and see if they have a heart-shaped one! And with mirrors on the ceiling!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (shouts to Chandler) And make sure our room isn't next to theirs (points to Phoebe).

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh! You guys are so lucky you are here with people, you known it's such a romantic place. That's all, I just wish I could (looks at Joey who is at the check in desk) share that with a guy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Not Joey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Not Joey, no, I was just lusting after Chandler.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, right!
[Cut to the guys]

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: So, ehm... I'm proposing to Pheobe tonight. (Removes a ring box from his pocket and opens it to show Chandler the ring)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Tonight?! (looks at the ring) Isn't an engagement ring supposed to have a diamond? (squints at the ring to emphasize how tiny the diamond is) Oh, there it is!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yeah, well, being a failed scientist doesn't pay quite as well as you might think. That's uhm... one seventieth of a karat. And the clarity is uhm... is quite poor.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (slaps him on the shoulder) Nice! (goes to Monica)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica, can I talk to you for a sec? (Pulls her away from Phoebe and Rachel)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: David is going to propose to Phoebe tonight!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: See what happens when you give people advice? I hope you told him not to?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That would be advice!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok fine. I'll handle this. (goes to Phoebe who's talking to Rachel) Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (looking very serious) I need to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you leaving "The Supremes"? (Monica and Phoebe go to one side)

lines.speaker: MOnica
lines.text: Ok, my husband just gave your boyfriend some very bad advice. Look, David is going to propose to you tonight.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow? Really? That's fantastic!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you serious? You wanna marry him? Wha... What about Mike?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, ok, you want me to marry Mike? Alright, well, let's just gag him and handcuff him and force him down the aisle. I can just see it: "Mike, do you take Phoebe..." (gestures with her hand as if someone is covering her mouth and tries to shout "No! No!") You know, it's every girl's dream!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you really think marrying someone else is the right answer?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure! Look, ok, bottom line: I love Mike... David! David. I love David. Don't look at me that way, Roseanne Rosannadanna!
Ross's hotel room. Ross and is reading his keynote speech to Charlie from his laptop

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: By using CT scans and computer imaging we can in a very real way, bring the Mesozoic era into the 21st century.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: It's great. You're gonna be the hit of the conference.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh and you know what, it will be even better tomorrow, because I won't be constantly interrupted by Joey checking to see if they put chocolates on my pillow yet. (Someone knocks on the door, Ross goes to open and it's Joey, Rachel and Chandler).

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey guys!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The chocolates aren't here yet.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Damnit!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Ross just read me his speech. It's fantastic!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, is it on the computer, cuz I'd love to give it a read...?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: If you want to check your email, just ask! (Chandler tries to look offended)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (offended) What? (pause) May I?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looking out the window) What's with the rain, Geller? I mean, when I signed up for Dino Week, nobody said anything about it being monsoon season.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Actually the wet season is June to December.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's not the time Charlie.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (at the laptop) Oh, no, no, no dear God, no!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh what, did someone outbid you for the teapot? (Chandler looks annoyed at him and Joey leans in to him) Oh! Secret teapot?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Your computer, I don't know wha... everything's gone!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wha... what do you mean? (Goes to the laptop)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It must be a virus. I think it erased your hard drive.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, oh my God. What did you do?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Someone I don't know sent me an e-mail and I opened it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why, why would you open it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it didn't say "This is a virus"!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What did it say?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nude... (Ross looks at him)... pictures of Anna Kournikova. I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What... what am I gonna do? My speech is gone, Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's not gone! I mean, I'm sure you printed out a copy. You have a hard copy, right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: NO! I don't!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you must be pretty mad at yourself right now...!
[Time lapse: Ross looks likes he's been trying to fix his computer but just closes it as if giving up]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's really gone?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yep! I'd like to thank you guys for coming down here to complain about the rain and ruin my career!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I just feel awful.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, well you should! I mean, nude pictures of Anna Kournikova? I mean, she's never even won a major tournament!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I tried Billy Jean King, but... (Ross glares at him) you know, you and Monica have the same "I'm gonna kill you" look...? I can usually make it go away by kissing her... (Ross continues to glare at him and Chandler leans in as if he's going to kiss Ross)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Get out! (Chandler runs out)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know, this happens all the time to my computer at work.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, what do you do?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I usually go... play Tetris on somebody else's computer.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe this. I can't believe this is happening. I have to give the keynote speech tomorrow! Ok? I have to stand up in front of all these people. What am I gonna say?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I could teach you a speech that I memorized for auditions.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't think that your monologue from Star Wars is gonna help me right now, Joe!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Ross, we can solve this. I just heard your speech. We can recreate it! We've got all night!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wha... what you really think we can do that?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh wait, Joey and I are supposed to have dinner (Looks at Joey).

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey don't worry about that! I mean, Ross needs you! And Rachel and I will stay and help anyway we can.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Alright, ok, let's do it. (Ross sits down at the desk and they all gather around him) Uhm, I know we start by discussing the shortcomings of carbon dating... uhm, and then, then I move on to what is clearly the defining moment of the Mesozoic era, the breakup of Pangea, hello! (Rachel and Joey look confused) And then, there's the... eh... there's the overview of the Triassic.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, oh! Any chance any of this happened in a "Galaxy far, far away"? (Ross turns aroud and glares at him. Joey and Rachel decide to leave).
Mike's apartment. His phone rings and he picks up

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, Mike, enough is enough, now you love Phoebe and she loves you, so you need to get over your whole "I never want to get married" thing and step up!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Who is this?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is Monica! I'm Phoebe's friend. Listen, Phoebe is back with David and he's going to propose to her, and she is going to say "yes" but I know she really wants to be with you!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (sounds shocked and sits down) He... he's gonna propose?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I... I'm sorry, did you say something? I can't hear through all this damned hair! (Tries to move her huge hair away from the phone, in vain)

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Look, if Phoebe wants to marry David, she should, I'm not gonna stand in the way of that and neither should you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You don't tell me what to do! I tell you what to do! Just call her. She's at the Paradise Hotel in Barbados. And while I've got you, you've got curly hair. What do you do in humidity? (Mike hangs up the phone) Damnit!
Chandler walks in

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (sarcastically) Well, I hope you're happy!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (pretending not to sense the tone) Oooh! I hope you're happy too, honey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe is going to say "Yes" to David. See, that's what happens when you meddle in people's lives!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Phoebe is going to say "yes"? That's, that's great!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No it's not, b'cause she's still in love with Mike!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And there's not chance that will work?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, I called him. It's not gonna happen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (pointing at her) Oooooooh! Meddler! Meddler!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, if you hadn't meddled to start with, I wouldn't have had to go in there and meddle myself. Now, no matter how much we meddle, we will never be able to un-meddle the thing that you meddled up - in the first place!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This vacation sucks!!
The hall, full of paleontologists. Rachel and Joey are walking around

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm so bored! Stupid rain, we... we can't do anything.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I've brought some books. We could read.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, it hasn't come to that yet.
A waiter walks by carrying appetizers or something on a tray

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (stopping the waiter) Hey hey hey! Don't mind if I do!

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: I'm sorry sir, these are for the pharmaceutical convention (walks away)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Rach, do you feel like going to a convention?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We can't. We're not pharmacists!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (walking to a table with many badges on it) I know we're not, but (he picks up a badge) Frank Medeio and... (picks up another badge) Eva Trorro... womba...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (picking up another random badge) Kate Miller?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Kate Miller it is. (he picks up the Kate Miller badge and sticks it on Rachel's breast)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And... that's the most sex I'm gonna have this weekend.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: In that case should I make sure it's on real good? (he does so, repeatedly tapping on her breast and stroking it)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you. (they walk away)
Ross's room. Charlie is sitting on the bed, while Ross is walking up and down nervously

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: And then, and then you said that thing about, about bringing the Mesozoic era in the 21st century.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, that's it?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God, we did it! (he sits beside her and skims through her notes excitedly)

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Actually I did it Ross. You remembered shockingly little of your own speech.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, but I did make a pyramid out of the bath products. This is amazing, thank you, thank you so much. (he leans in, and they look at each other for a moment) That's a pretty necklace.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, what do you say we celebrate? Champagne? (he goes to get the champagne)

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh yeah! Hey, save the cork and then we can fill the bottle with water and put it back so they don't charge you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God, I love you.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh, this is such a cute picture of Emma. And is this your son... or just some kid whose picture you bring on vacation?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's Ben, my son from my first marriage.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Your first marriage?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: You're married more than once?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. (they clink glasses and drink)

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: So, why did you break up?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (embarassed) Oh, it was... it's complicated, you know? She... she was... eh... gay.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh my God, this is so cool!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, odd thing to get excited about!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: No, it's just... I was enganged to a guy who turned out to be gay!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! High-five! (they high-five)

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Didn't you feel so stupid that you didn't see the signs? My fiance was always going away on these long weekends with his tennis partner.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My wife had a workout friend she went to the gym with everyday for a year. She didn't get any fitter.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Right and then everybody finds out and they're like: "Oh, I knew all along"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know! It's like, if you knew, why didn't you tell me, you know? I mean, call, or leave a note: "Hi, I just dropped by to say your wife's gay"

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And then, you try to make the best of a bad situation, so you float the idea of a threesome?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I didn't do that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (embarassed) Me neither.
the Pharmacist convention. Joey and Rachel are walking out of it, drinking cocktails

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, who knew? Pharmacists are fun.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, that old lady at the end was ready to take you home.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Not enough pills in the world, Rach. What about you, you're the single one, seen anybody in there you like?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, let's see. There was a really big guy that I was talking to, with the really nice breasts...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But what about back home, anything going on there? Anybody you like?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (takes a sip from her drink, embarassed) No.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: There it is, you're blushing!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I'm not blushing, I'm sunburnt! From, you know, the rain.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You like someone. Tell me who it is. Who is it? (tickles her a little)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Tell me who it is.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey! (she walks away; Joey goes after her teasing and tickling her)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on who? Who do you like? Tell me. You're not getting away that easy. Who do you like, who?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, come on! It doesn't matter, you know, it's not like anything's gonna happen.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? Why not? Rach, who can you not get?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! (pause) Ok. Ok, you really wanna know who it is?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (eagerly) Yeah, who is that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do ya?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.
Ross and Charlie walk into the hall from Ross's room

lines.speaker: Ross and Charlie
lines.text: Hey!
Joey smiles at them. Rachel looks annoyed

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: (to Joey) I just left you a message! Ross and I were gonna go grab a bite, but now that you're here, maybe we can go have that dinner.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right, of course. Hey, did you guys finish the speech?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yep, we got it, we got it. (To Charlie) Thank you so much.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I had a great time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright, hey look, and this isn't over, because I really wanna know who...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Later! La...

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: So, shall we?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. (they leave)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok. See you, bye.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good night.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Night.
Ross and Rachel watch them walk away and sigh. They look at each other, embarassed.

lines.speaker: Ross and Rachel
lines.text: Ok, good night!
The restaurant. Chandler and Monica are sitting at a table
Phoebe and David walk in

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe she's gonna say yes to David. She's clearly in love with Mike.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, it's very hard to take you seriously when you look like that.
David and Phoebe sit down at a table close to Chandler and Monica's

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Uh, Phoebe, uh, I have... something I wanna say.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, he's gonna do it now. Please, I cannot watch this, let's go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think we have some time. Have you ever heard him talk? (doing David) "Uh, Phoebe, uh, I would be honoured, uh..." Spit it out, David!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Uh, Phoebe, uh... (Chandler hits his own head) you're an amazing woman, and the time we spent apart was, was unbearable. Of course the sanitation strikes in Minsk didn't help!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure, ok, yeah.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: But well, now that we're together again, I don't ever want to be apart. So, to that end...
David produces the ring. At the same time, Mike walks in, behind David

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God, Mike!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: It's David, actually!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, Mike's here.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: (turns around) Hi Mike!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Hi David. Chandler. Monica... (Looks at Monica, checking her big hair, aghast) Oh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: IT'S THE HUMIDITY!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Hi Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What are you, what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I have a question I need to ask you.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: I have a question I was kinda gonna ask her myself.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah, I understand, but before you do, she really needs to hear this.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: (annoyed) Ok, would you care for my seat as well?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Actually yeah, that'll be great.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: That's fair, you've had a long trip. (he leaves his seat to Mike, and stands there looking for a chair. He finally goes to Monica and Chandler's table)

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Phoebe, I love you. I mean, I missed you so much these last few months and I thought we were apart for a good reason, but then I suddenly realized that there was no reason good enough to keep me from spending the rest of my life with you.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Kinda stepped on the toes of what I was going to say.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Sorry David, but she really has to know this.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Alright, but after this I want to see you outside. If the rain stops.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're the most incredible woman I've ever met. How can I lose you? (Phoebe looks very flattered) Now, I don't actually have a ring...

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: I have a ring.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I wouldn't brag too much about that thing, big guy.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Phoebe, will you marry me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (smiles at him happily for a few seconds before answering) No!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Uhm... Ha ha!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I love you. But I never needed a proposal from you. I just needed to know that we were headed somewhere, you know, that we had a future.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: We can have any future you want.
they hold their hands, gazing at each other

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Ok, I'm gonna take off.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: David, I'm so sorry, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Just so I know, if I had asked first...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I might have said yes, but that would have been wrong.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Please, you don't have to explain. I mean, perhaps if I hadn't gone to Minsk things would have worked out for us. And I wouldn't have ruined my career, or lost that toe to frostbite. It was a good trip! (he leaves)

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Is it ok if I hug you now?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes! (they hug)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to everybody) BECAUSE OF OUR MEDDLING! Alright?
Monica and Chandler's room. Monica and Chandler are in bed.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, ain't this nice? It's so quiet, I could just lie here all day.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know (she snuggles to him)
Rachel runs in

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (walking in hurriedly) Open your drapes! Open your drapes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm so glad we've got adjoining rooms!
Rachel opens the drapes

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The sun is out!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! Remember when I had corneas?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok listen, you go down to the pool and reserve the chairs, and I'll get the magazines and the lotion.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ladies? Ross's speech is in 45 minutes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nooo!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Damn it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (from across the wall) Walls are pretty thin, guys!
Conference room. Ross is making his keynote speech

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Then we have to await the data from recent MRI scans and DNA testing which call into question information gathered from years of simple carbon dating.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look at that woman sitting by the pool getting tan... so leathery and wrinkled, I'm so jealous!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Finally, factoring the profusion of new species recently discovered: Gigantosaurus, Argentinasaurus...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to a paleontologist sitting next to him) Not to mention the cold sores.
the paleontologist glares at Chandler

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And that's just the herbivores. I'm not even gonna discuss the carnivores, their heads are already too big. Which is ironic considering their stunted cerebral development.
all the paleontologists laugh

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to the one sitting next to him) Really?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But all kidding aside, in much the same way that Homo ergaster [1] is now thought to be a separate species from Homo erectus...
Joey laughs

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: He said "erectus"!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: You're... you're kidding, right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, he really said it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ... and while there are certainly vast differences between these Mesozoic fossiles and the example of Homo erectus...
Rachel laughs

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Erectus?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Homo.
the hotel conference room

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (concluding his speech)... in a very real way we can bring the Mesozoic era into the 21st century. (pauses) Thank you!
Everybody stands up and applauds. Ross looks flattered and surprised. His friends and other members of the audience go to congratulate him

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, thanks guys!

lines.speaker: Man with a bow tie
lines.text: (shaking hands with Ross) I thought... it was wonderful!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Man with a bow tie
lines.text: Jarvis Oberblau, Cornell. (sighs) I mean, the ideas you put forth and, and from someone... so... young... and... (sighs again and smiles at Ross blissfully).

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (with a frozen smile on his face, realizing something's wrong with Jarvis) Ok... now... now we're just holding hands! (pulls his hand away)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right! Well, uh... (to Monica) we're gonna hit the beach?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Ross, in a flattering tone) It was really... great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, thank you so much!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, and so funny!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!
Rachel, Joey and Chandler pat him on his shoulders and walk off, together with Monica

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (puzzled) Ok!... All right, thanks! Thank you so much, you guys! (to Phoebe) Oh, I can't tell you how much it means to me that you were here!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (popping by, smiling) You're kidding, we wouldn't have missed it!
Ross is dumbfounded to see Mike instead of David

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Oh... I'm back!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (skating over, embarrassed) Ok!... Uh... excuse me? Yeah?

lines.speaker: Phoebe & Mike
lines.text: Yeah! (they leave)
Ross goes towards Charlie, who's conversing with a fellow paleontologist, and touches her shoulder to get her attention

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! (she turns to him) Well...? (in expectation)

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: You were incredible!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: You blew them away!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I can't tell you how great it was to look at the crowd and see your face! I mean... uh, did you know you were (giggles) mouthing the words along with me?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: (smiling broadly) I was not!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, it's ok! Made me feel like a rock star!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh my God! (pauses) I'm your groupie!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (joking) I'd better not found you naked in my hotel room!
Ross giggles, but Charlie isn't amused at all.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (realizing his joke wasn't so good, but still giggling) Look, I took it too far!
the hotel lobby. Monica, Phoebe, Chandler and Mike walk in from the outside.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (her hair bigger then before) I can't believe it's raining again! Oh, it's so unfair!!!
They approach the buffet, where a couple of paleontologists are sipping their drinks

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, on the bright side, now you won't have to see all these paleontologists with their shirts off. (Grabs a drink and notices that the two men are upset) Not you guys. You got it going on!
Monica, Phoebe, Chandler and Mike walk away, sipping their drinks

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, what are we gonna do today?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: They have a game room downstairs! Ping pong and stuff.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (pleasantly surprised) Ping pong? (to Chandler) Honey, they have ping pong! Let's play!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't think so!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (disappointed) Why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because you know how competitive you get and well, I say it's cute, others disagree, and I'm lying!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm not always that bad!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, yeah? What happened when we played last time?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (hesitatingly) I punched you...?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And...?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: ... Phoebe...?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I clunked your heads together!
Chandler turns to Mike and gives him a "See what I mean?" look
Joey and Charlie's room

Joey is sitting in an armchair and wearing a diving mask. He pulls out a grape from a bunch of fake grapes on the coffee table, puts it on the snorkel's breathing tube and blows it out, then giggles to himself

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: (walking in) Hey! There you are!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! (stands up) As soon as it stops raining we have got to go snorkeling! Some kid told me about the sea turtle and, if you blow bubbles in its face, it chases ya! (smiles ecstatic)

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I'm sorry, I can't! I'm running a discussion group all afternoon.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (disappointed) Oh... oh, but that's ok, I'll find someone else to do it... I'll do it alone, but... I don't know what happens if the sea turtle catches you...

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: You know... I feel so bad! I haven't seen you this whole trip and (pauses) especially last night...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (interrupting her) Hey! Don't worry about it! It was fine! I ended up having the best time with Rachel! I just felt bad for you, stuck in that room, working on Ross's speech... (pulls a face)

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Actually, it turned out to be a lot of fun!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (bewildered) Oh! Oh, well! At least we're both having fun!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Yeah...
There's an awkward moment of silence

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: ... is it weird that it's not with each other?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! A little bit, yeah...

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: (sitting down on the bed) I think we need to talk...!
pause

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah... I think we do... (sighs, with folded arms)... about what?
the hotel game room. There is a ping pong table in the middle of the room. Monica, Phoebe, Chandler and Mike walk in

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: C'mon guys, it'll be fun!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, all right... I'll play if we don't keep score!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But then how do we know who wins?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nobody wins!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, we're just four losers... SUPER!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm not playing with you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I'm out.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I'll play ya!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (smiling) OK!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mike, you don't know, you don't know what you're doing!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She gets crazy! This scar (points to his forehead) is from Pictionary!
Monica rolls her eyes

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (disbelieving) I think I will be all right! (to Monica) You wanna volley a bit for a serve?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sure! Got to!
Monica and Mike start to play ping pong. Mike scores

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aww!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Oh, by the way... I'm awesome!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (nearly whispering) Oh dear God, there's two of them!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: You're ready to play?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hell, yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Did you know this about him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No idea! I though he was soft like you!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Wanna make it more interesting?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How much were you thinking?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Ten bucks a game?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Make it fifty!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I'll make it a hundred!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (nearly shouting) One thousand...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (interrupting her) OK!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: To see who goes first, you got a quarter?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (going through her pockets) No... (to Chandler and Phoebe) Either of you girls got a quarter?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Honey, try to focus the trash talk on him!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (picks up a coin from her bra) Monica, you call it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Heads! No, Tails! He-he-heads!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Tails!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (angry) Ow, what are the chances!
They start playing again

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ha! My point!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Oh, no! I don't think so! You know, according to standard table tennis rules if at any time a player uses his non racket bearing hand to touch the playing surface he or she forfeits the point.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (smiling proudly) He was a lawyer!
Rachel's hotel room. She is watching the Weather Channel on TV.

lines.speaker: Alexandra Steele
lines.text: (meteorologist) (pointing to the East Coast)... all these coasts having beautiful weather. In New York, it's 72 and sunny!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Weather bitch! (turns the TV off)
Someone knocks on the door

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's open! (Joey walks in) Hi, Joe!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (downhearted) Hey...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (worried) What, is everything ok?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh... Charlie and I broke up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nooooo, why?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh well, she said we have nothing in common.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughing) Oh, that's crazy!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, it's not, we have nothing in common!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ... yeah, it's true.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I mean, she should be with someone like... Ross! You know what I mean, he uses all those big words too! Man, smart people are dull!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (pretending to be offended) What, hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (laughing sarcastically) Ok, Rach!
He punches her on her shoulder mockingly, then goes and sits down on her bed

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I feel so stupid, you know? Why... why do I keep going after the wrong girls?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: W-What are you, what are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, c'mon, I mean, there's you, then there's Charlie, and it's like... (sighs) What the hell is my problem? OH! (He falls back on the bed)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok... uh... maybe you're not always going after the wrong girl...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sitting up again) I'm telling you, Rach, Charlie is not right for me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I'm not talking about her...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But then who? The waitress I went out with last month? (gives her a meaningful look)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know? Forget it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (stands up) No-no-no-no, no! Who, who were you talking about?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I-I-I-I don't, I actually don't know who I'm talking about! So!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok! All right, well... I'm gonna see if I can get a room for the night and I'll... I'll see you later!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, sure!

Joey walks out, while Rachel is pensive. Once he's out of her room, he suddenly realizes who she was talking about and goes back in. He looks at her in disbelief and she looks like she was caught red-handed
Rachel's hotel room. Joey is standing at the door, facing Rachel

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You like me? (shuts the door)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (nearly whispering) Ok, let's not make a big thing about this!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (shocked) That's a huge thing!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Not working with me, Joe! Here's the thing: lately I have been having thoughts (pauses) musings, if you will!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What... for how long?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Only like a month!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (outraged) A MONTH??

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What the... DIAL IT DOWN! (Joey goes to sit on the bed) Listen, ok, and maybe they're crazy thoughts, but sometimes I do, I have, I've been thinking about... you know, us! (looks at Joey, who's totally distraught) Ok, dial it up a little!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (stands up) I just have one question!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Shoot!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (desperate) What the hell are you doin'???

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know, I'm not trying to do anything, it's just, we have such a good time when we're together, you know... I mean, aren't you just a... little curious... (insinuating) what that would be like?...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, am I curious? I mean, I am as curious as... as... George!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (puzzled) Who...?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: CURIOUS GEORGE (see link)! You know, the monkey, and the guy with the yellow hat!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yes, of course, I remember him!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, he had a paper route.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, he did! (smiling) Oh, see, this is what I'm talking about!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I know, yeah I know we're great but Rach no... this... this can't happen!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But can it... just... happen a little bit?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (charmed, but then recoiling) NO, NO! It can't happen at all!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But why, why not?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Because... look, no one wants this to happen more than me, ok? (in a trembling voice) I have gone over this moment in my head a hundred times and not once did I ever say no! (sighs) I couldn't do it to Ross!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But that wasn't gonna stop you before!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know, I know! But I've thought about it a lot since, and it just wouldn't be right... (painfully) I'm sorry...!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (regretful) I'm sorry, too! (they look at each other sadly, then she recollects, and puts her hands over her eyes) OH GOD! I shouldn't have said anything!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: NO! No-no-no-no-no-no! Hey! Hey, we'll be fine! Li... hey, like you said: no big deal!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's not a big deal!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: NO BIG DEAL!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's so not a big deal!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! I'll see ya later! Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok!
They shake hands, he walks out and shuts the door, then seems to change his mind, moves to open the door, than changes his mind again and leans over the door. Just then, Rachel opens the door

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, I...
Joey falls backwards into the room

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: AAAHHHH!
Joey hurriedly stands up, arms akimbo, gives her an embarrassed look and walks away
the hotel game room. Monica and Mike are still playing ping pong

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ooh! I'm sorry! I think, I THINK, that may have missed the table!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Do you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ah, yeah!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Do you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ah, yeaaah!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: DO YOU?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: AH YEAAAAH!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Do you really find this attractive on him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (looking at Mike) Oh, yeah! (turning to Chandler) Are you telling me you... you're not even... a little turned on by Monica, right now?
Chandler turns to look at Monica, who has the biggest hair ever, is flushed and in a sweat, and is decidedly sniffing her armpits

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think this is the first time in our marriage that I've felt like the more attractive one.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: C'mon Mike, you can beat her! Knock that dog off her head!
Mike scores

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, damn it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (pointing at Mike and shouting) I sleep with him!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (boasting) Game, point!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (threatening) Don't get too cocky! Remember I won the last one! Oh, by the way, how did that feel, losing to a girl?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: You know, you should really look in a mirror before you call yourself that.
they continue to play ping pong and then Mike scores, winning the game

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: NO, NO, NOOO!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: And that's how it's done! (Phoebe kisses him)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay-dokay, you've each won a game and I've lost what's felt like a year of my life. So everybody goes home a winner.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Best out of three?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: That's what I'm thinking.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Should I use my invisibility to fight crime or for evil?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Mike) Serve the ball, chump!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (doing Monica and mumbling): Serve the ball, chump.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Mike) Ok Mike, better come back Mike, better come back.
hotel's bar, Ross and Mr. Oberblau are talking

lines.speaker: Mr. Oberblau
lines.text: I'm just saying, I have a cabin in the Adirondacks if you ever want to get away from the city, well, that'd be (pause) just nifty!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ooh, well. Ah, I kinda have got a lot on my plate right now, not that I wouldn't love a weekend in the country with a strange man. (Mr. Oberblau giggles)

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Jarvis?

lines.speaker: Mr. Oberblau
lines.text: (seeing her) Oh, you're back... (to Ross) this is my wife, Nancy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Get Out!
Charlie walks by

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Ross, can I talk to you for a minute?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, please! (they move and sit down on a sofa) So, what's going on?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Uh, well... Joey and I broke up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God, wh-what happened?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Joey is a great guy, but we're just... so different! I mean, during your speech he kept laughing at homo erectus!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I knew that was him!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Anyway I just, uh, I think it's for the best.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (holding her hand) Hey, you ok?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I guess. There was hum... (she breathes deeply) there was another reason that I thought it was time to end it with Joey. I started to realize that I was having feelings for someone (pause) else.
some paleontologists interrupt them

lines.speaker: Paleontologist
lines.text: (merrily) Ok Geller. Last day of the conference, you know what happens to the keynote speaker.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, professor Clerk we're kind of in the middle of a conversation, here.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Yeah, can you guys just throw him in the pool later?

lines.speaker: Professore Clerk
lines.text: Or we could throw you both in now!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (standing) Ok, gentlemen! Please! Aren't we a little old for this? I mean, we're scientists, right? We're academics. And most importantly I... you-you will have to catch us first. (he starts to run away with Charlie). GO, GO, GO! (the paleontologists starts chasing them)
game room, Monica and Mike are still playing ping pong
Chandler and Phoebe look bored to death. Monica scores and laughs

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Ok, so it's a tie again, 41 to 41.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (exhausted) Ok, look! Enough is enough!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, I have just to have two more points to beat him!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica, that was also true an hour ago! I mean, please, look at you! Your hand is blistered, you can barely stand, your hair is inexplicable! Ok, you've already proven you are just as good as he is, now we've missed our dinner reservations, so now let's just go upstairs, order room service, take a shower and shave your head!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't just walk away! I've put in four hours!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look! You knew this about me when you married me! You agreed to take me in sickness and in health. Well, this is my sickness!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What about the obsessive cleaning?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's just good sense!
they start playing again; suddenly Monica hits the table with her hand

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (in pain) Aww! (she holds her hand, moaning like she's biting back a scream)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You ok?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, no, no. Honey, I'm ok. Shake it off! (she shakes the wrist and it's more painful) Oh, no! No shaking, no shaking! Ooh! Ooh! (pause) Oh my God! I can't play!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: So you forfeit?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mike wins?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe it! (pause) I lost!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, you didn't.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because I'm gonna play for ya.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You can't do that!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Oh, that's ok. I don't care which of them I beat.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, we're taking that paddle home, mister.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Chandler) Honey, you don't have to do this.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (In a loving voice) Yes, I do. Now, I may not understand why you have to win so badly, but if it's important to you then it's important to me, because I love you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But... you suck!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Still in a loving voice) You're welcome, sweetheart.
Chandler prepares to play

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right Mike, let's get this over with. Sudden death. Whoever wins this point, wins.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Ok!
They start playing and Chandler does not suck at all

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! You're good!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's like watching porn!
Chandler scores and wins the match

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And that's... how... it's done!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Chandler) Oh my God. That was so amazing! When did you (pause) Hold on! I almost forgot (she turns to Mike) loser! (back to Chandler) When did you stop sucking?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I never sucked, I actually didn't want you to know how good I was!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is so great! Now we can enter into doubles tournaments!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's why!
Hotel's bar. Ross is running to Charlie trying not to be seen with two cocktails in his hands. She's hidden behind a huge plant

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Are they still looking for us?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. The bartender said that they split up into two search parties, the herbivores and the carnivores. (pause) You know, we as a group are not the coolest.
Three paleontologists walk by and Ross hugs Charlie trying not to be seen

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't think they saw us.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I don't think they did.
They realize that they are hugging closely and he draws back

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Hum, so, I started to say you something earlier, hum... (pause) There was another reason I realized it was time to end it with Joey. I kind of realized I... was starting to have feelings... for someone else.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (apparently unruffled) Oh. Can I... can I ask who?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I think you know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think I know too but I've been really wrong about this stuff in the past, so...
Charlie kisses Ross, they stop for a moment and then he kisses her back

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sorry... we... we can't.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: All right, all right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I mean, you just went out with my best friend, and I just think it'd be a really really bad idea. (pause) Or-or not! (they kiss passionately)
Joey walks in and sees Ross and Charlie kissing. He gives a faint, rueful smile, then he seems to recollect something and suddenly he moves back to Rachel's room. He knocks on her door and she opens

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

Joey says nothing, but enters the room and kisses her. They are kissing passionately only to stop for a brief "oh" from Rachel. They continue their passionate kiss and Joey closes the door with his foot and it shuts in the camera's "face". And that's the end of the ninth season.
End

[1] Homo ergaster: Some scientists classify some African erectus specimens as belonging to a separate species, Homo ergaster, which differs from the Asian H. erectus fossils in some details of the skull (e.g. the brow ridges differ in shape, and erectus would have a larger brain size).
Season 9
